ksa_text: Ability to develop and implement data and AI governance policies and procedures that operationalize organizational governance frameworks.

dcwf.top_element_label: Assist in the development and refinement of priority information requirements.

ddat.top_element_label: Capability building for digital, data and technology

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: AI risks based on assessments and other analytical output from the MAP and MEASURE functions are prioritized, responded to, and managed.

top_anchors.element_label: Capability building for digital, data and technology
Assist in the development and refinement of priority information requirements.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Although Data Governance is an edge domain for the Director of Data Science, this ability KSA presents sufficient signal (composite score 0.471) and conceptual alignment to include at the director level.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (Data Governance), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Pass 1 signal is moderate (composite 0.471). External reviewers may question whether this specific ability KSA is sufficiently distinctive for the Director of Data Science versus adjacent roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: DAMA-DMBOK, EDM Council, and enterprise CDO job descriptions universally include data governance competencies for data leadership roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Overturn: while the domain is relevant, this specific ability KSA is better attributed to a more specialized adjacent role. Revised to exclude.

decision_rationale: Initially included but overturned through adversarial review. Data Governance ability KSA does not meet the quality or scope threshold for the Director of Data Science after cross-framework validation.
